test_cases.title: Overdue indicator displayed when current date is past due date and task is not done
preconditions: A task exists with a due date set to a past date (e.g., yesterday)
The task status is not 'done' (e.g., 'in progress' or 'open')
steps: Retrieve or view the task whose due date is in the past and status is not 'done'
Observe whether an overdue indicator is present on the task
test_cases.expected_result: The overdue indicator is displayed on the task.

test_cases.title: No overdue indicator displayed when task status is 'done' and due date is in the past
preconditions: A task exists with a due date set to a past date
steps: Retrieve or view the task whose due date is in the past and status is 'done'
Observe whether an overdue indicator is present on the task
test_cases.expected_result: The overdue indicator is NOT displayed on the task.

test_cases.title: No overdue indicator displayed when due date is today (current date equals due date)
preconditions: A task exists with a due date set to today's date
steps: Retrieve or view the task whose due date equals today's date and status is not 'done'
Observe whether an overdue indicator is present on the task
test_cases.expected_result: The overdue indicator is NOT displayed on the task, since the current date is not past (strictly after) the due date.

test_cases.title: No overdue indicator displayed when task has no due date set
preconditions: A task exists with no due date assigned (due date field is empty/null)
steps: Retrieve or view the task with no due date and a non-done status
Observe whether an overdue indicator is present on the task
test_cases.expected_result: The overdue indicator is NOT displayed on the task.

test_cases.title: No overdue indicator displayed when due date is in the future and task is not done
preconditions: A task exists with a due date set to a future date (e.g., tomorrow)
steps: Retrieve or view the task whose due date is in the future and status is not 'done'
Observe whether an overdue indicator is present on the task
test_cases.expected_result: The overdue indicator is NOT displayed on the task.

test_cases.title: Overdue indicator removed when an overdue task is marked as 'done'
preconditions: A task exists with a due date set to a past date
The task status is not 'done' and the overdue indicator is currently displayed
steps: Retrieve or view the updated task
Observe whether the overdue indicator is present on the task
test_cases.expected_result: The overdue indicator is no longer displayed after the task status is changed to 'done'.

assumptions: The overdue condition uses strict inequality: the current date must be strictly after (not equal to) the due date to trigger the overdue indicator.
Due date granularity is at the day level (date only, not date-time); if time is involved, boundary behavior may differ.
All non-'done' statuses (e.g., 'open', 'in progress', 'blocked') are treated equivalently for overdue evaluation.
The overdue indicator is a single, identifiable element or attribute on the task representation (e.g., a flag, label, or field) that can be deterministically observed.
notes: The requirement does not define what 'overdue indicator' looks like (e.g., UI badge, API boolean field, color change) — test steps are intentionally generic. The exact comparison operator for 'past the due date' (strictly greater than vs. greater than or equal to) is ambiguous; the boundary test case for today's date assumes strict inequality but this should be confirmed. The requirement does not specify behavior when the due date is set to a date in the past at the time of task creation — this scenario is assumed to be handled the same as any other overdue task. No permission or security behaviors are specified for this requirement.
